description_long: This object is a classic Motorola flip phone, featuring a clamshell design with a prominent hinge at the center. The front section houses a color display screen with a visible Motorola logo and a circular interface, while the lower half contains a tactile keypad with raised buttons and a central navigation pad. The exterior back panel includes a small secondary display and a circular metallic emblem near the hinge. The phone's body is primarily black with metallic silver accents, and the hinge mechanism is robust and visually distinct. The device is compact, lightweight, and constructed from a combination of plastic and metal, typical of early 2000s mobile phones.
description: A black Motorola flip phone with a color display, tactile keypad, and metallic hinge, featuring both main and secondary screens.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open phone with a color display, Motorola logo, and full keypad layout.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile, visible hinge, and the separation between the upper and lower halves.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel with a small secondary screen and a circular metallic emblem near the hinge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the hinge and the phone's thin, clamshell construction.
attribute_brand_logo: A blue and white Motorola 'M' logo is visible on the upper display screen, and a circular metallic Motorola emblem is present near the hinge on the back panel.
attribute_screen_display_content: The main screen displays a Motorola-themed wallpaper with the text 'HELLOMOTO', circular icons, and a blue and white color scheme. The secondary screen on the back shows a small, unclear image or notification.
attribute_camera_placement: No visible camera modules or lenses are present on any side of the phone.
attribute_button_layout: The phone features a full alphanumeric keypad with a central navigation pad below the main screen, and several small side buttons are visible along the edges near the hinge.
